items.title: How an insect repellent left almost a dozen hospital staff sick

children.content: How did an insect repellent get to the hospital? Who released it? And how did almost a dozen staff at the hospital get sick?

articles.title: Insect repellent release leaves 11 staff sick at major SA hospital

children.content: Eleven staff at an Adelaide hospital have become unwell and a dozen emergency beds have been closed, after the accidental release of an "outdoor insect repellent" inside the facility.

image.altText: A fire truck at the Flinders Medical Centre.

gallery.altText: The scene of an incident at the Flinders Medical Centre.

thumbnail.altText: The scene of an incident at the Flinders Medical Centre.

items.title: Divers map underwater caves that weave under city streets

children.content: Cave divers have swapped measuring tapes and pencils for 3D modelling to map a patchwork of underwater caves below houses and streets in Mount Gambier.

image.altText: a diver with air tanks and a helmet holding a pole with cameras on it in front of him

items.title: Adelaide now fourth most expensive capital for buying a house

children.content: Data shows Adelaide is bucking national housing trends with median property prices rising 4.8 per cent in the June quarter.

image.altText: A picture of a sold housing sign, with a hand placed over the sold sticker.

items.title: SA police say man dies while resisting arrest in Adelaide CBD

children.content: A man has died in the Adelaide CBD after police say he became unresponsive during an attempted arrest this afternoon.

gallery.altText: Police blocking area near a pub and hotel

thumbnail.altText: Police blocking area near a pub and hotel

items.title: MotoGP to rev up motorcycle culture and pilgrimages to City of Churches

children.content: The arrival of the Tour Down Under in 1999 inspired a significant uptick in Lycra-wearing cyclists in and around Adelaide. Will the MotoGP have a similar effect for high-performance motorcycles?

image.altText: Man on stationary motorcycle inside a store

items.title: 'Fascinating world': McRae questions booing directed at Quaynor

children.content: Collingwood franks its strong run of form with an impressive 34-point win over Adelaide, as Crows fans defy pleas from the club and boo Isaac Quaynor.

image.altText: GettyImages-2286661073

items.title: Labor warned 'sleeper issue' driving One Nation support

children.content: The ALP's 50th National Conference kicked off with the rise of One Nation, local council budgets and regional voter frustrations leading negotiations.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese smiles while standing at a podium. The words "Delivering Real Change" appear on the podium and on the wall

items.title: Billions in new funding for AUKUS shipyards in Adelaide

children.content: The billions of dollars in additional funding is another step towards making Australia's nuclear submarine program "a reality".

image.altText: Richard Marles wear black suit jacket, white shirt and blue with pink dotted tie.

items.title: Family takes racing club to court over 'enormous' hidden rubbish dump

children.content: A family discovered a large waste dump, allegedly created by a South Australian horse racing club, as they began foundation work on their home, court documents allege.

image.altText: A woman and man standing together with a rubbish pit behind them.

items.title: Former MP convicted of three extra counts of deception

children.content: Former SA state MP Fraser Ellis now stands convicted of seven counts of deception after failing to have his original convictions thrown out on appeal, and having several acquittals overturned.

image.altText: Fraser Ellis outside court

items.title: Kiana started weightlifting after an injury. Now, she's a Comm Games athlete

children.content: Kiana Elliott thought gymnastics would be her pathway to the podium, but turned to weightlifting after an injury forced her to retire.

gallery.altText: Kiana Elliott at a gym, bending down with her hands on weights as she prepares to lift them. She is dressed in a blue t-shirt

thumbnail.altText: Kiana Elliott at a gym, bending down with her hands on weights as she prepares to lift them. She is dressed in a blue t-shirt

items.title: Chinese diplomats leave ALP conference after bid to eject Taiwan officials

children.content: The incident could stir fresh tensions between China and Australia in the wake of several recent high-profile disputes.

image.altText: Close up of a man in a dark suit in a crowd

items.title: Indigenous-owned town hopes giant fish statue will attract tourists

children.content: On the far west coast of SA, this Aboriginal community requires a permit to enter, meaning people usually drive on by. But new public art at the roadhouse, bought by the community, aims to boost tourism and financial independence.

image.altText: A large sculpture of a fish is on stilts in a shed setting

items.title: Adelaide man charged with producing AI child exploitation material

children.content: SA Police have arrested and charged a man from Adelaide's northern suburbs for the production and possession of AI-generated child exploitation material.

image.altText: An SA police car parked on the side of a street with trees on the opposite side

items.title: AFL's first publicly gay player calls for respect in Crows-Pies match

children.content: Four months after becoming the first current or former AFL player to publicly come out as gay, Leigh Ryswyk is urging fans not to boo the Collingwood player targeted by Crow Izak Rankine’s homophobic slur.

image.altText: Leigh Ryswyk Header image

images.altText: King Charles at Commonwealth Games opening ceremony
Team Australia arrives at the 2026 Commonwealth Games
Athletes from Team Australia arrive at the 2026 Commonwealth Games

articles.title: Out of necessity is borne a unique and wholly Scottish opening ceremony

children.content: The 2026 Commonwealth Games are like no other that came before them and the opening ceremony was similarly unique in a way that will make it hard to go back.

image.altText: A silhouetted bag piper at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony.

articles.title: Glasgow was asleep and the Games weren't open as Australia got its first win

children.content: Australia's first victory of the Commonwealth Games comes in a surprise extra session as the bowlers start before the opening ceremony.

image.altText: Kelsey Cottrell high fives Australian women's pairs bowls teammate Dawn Hayman at the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games.

articles.title: Your guide to every day of the Comm Games in Australian time

children.content: With 10 sports crammed into 10 full days of action in Glasgow, it may be hard to keep track of what's on and when at the Commonwealth Games. So, here's the full schedule of every day of the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games with all times and days in AEST.

image.altText: A basketballer spins a ball on his finger, a boxer poses, a weightlifter holds up a barbell, and a wheelchair basketballer.

children.content: The Commonwealth Games are officially opened by King Charles III as many of the 3,000 athletes parade in front of 14,000 spectators. Follow live.

items.title: Commonwealth Games open with tartan, a unicorn and the King in a TARDIS

updates.title: The opening ceremony is over. The 2026 Commonwealth Games have begun!
King Charles officially declares the Games open!

items.title: Automakers in shock as China takes over

children.content: In June, a bulk carrier made an unprecedented delivery of EVs to Australia, sounding an ominous warning to the world's automakers.

image.altText: A large red and white ship sits in a dock as a white car is driven off its ramp

items.title: 'Far more' needs to be done to help children leaving state care

children.content: Queensland Family and Children's Commissioner Luke Twyford says the situation is "profoundly upsetting".

image.altText: A pile of personal belongings stacked up on the street, next to a homeless person in a sleeping bag

items.title: Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump

children.content: Donald Trump is insisting the US deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear energy industry has one major condition — the Arab kingdom must formally recognise the State of Israel.

articles.title: Trump went to war to restrict a nuclear program. Now he's backing one

children.content: Donald Trump went to war with Tehran to stop it enriching nuclear fuel. Now he's approved a deal that analysts warn could open the door for another Middle Eastern power to potentially do exactly that.

image.altText: Donald Trump points at a wall while walking with Mohammed bin Salman.

articles.title: US and Saudi Arabia reach nuclear power deal

children.content: Israeli officials and US politicians from both parties have voiced concern that a civilian nuclear project in Saudi Arabia could be used to eventually develop nuclear weapons.

image.altText: Donald Trump holding hands with Mohammed Bin Salman during a meeting in the Oval Office.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Petrol supply could 'tighten' as oil jumps over $US100 a barrel

children.content: The entry of the Houthi rebels into the war between the US and Iran has threatened another vital shipping route through the Middle East.

image.altText: Cars queue at a petrol station.

items.title: First nursery for babies of Ebola victims opens in Congo

children.content: The first UNICEF-supported Ebola nursery opens in the Democratic Republic of Congo, supporting young children whose parents have contracted the disease while the number of cases intensifies.

image.altText: Mapenzi, 17 years old, with her baby brother Eliya, 3 months old.

items.title: Small business insolvency rates begin to rise

children.content: Small-to-medium sized businesses have had a challenging year, with increased costs and regulatory reforms. Insolvency rates are beginning to rise and more owners are shopping around for credit.

gallery.altText: Jeff and Kerry-Ann Bowman stand outside a store

thumbnail.altText: Jeff and Kerry-Ann Bowman stand outside a store

items.title: After Brat, Charli xcx asks a bigger question about legacy

children.content: On her new album Music, Fashion, Film, British artist Charli xcx discusses the enduring legacy of art and artists, and how creative figures are remembered long after their moment has passed.

image.altText: Charli xcx stands on a red carpet in a black dress surrounded by fans holding green records

items.title: Adolescence creator tackles teen rehab very differently in The Good Boy

children.content: Emmy Award-winning Adolescence co-creator Stephen Graham tackles teen delinquency in a very different way in The Good Boy.

items.title: Friday news quiz: World Cup winners, rock legends and Barnaby Joyce's dog

children.content: We have 10 questions to test your knowledge of the week in … SIT DOWN! Sorry, it's the ABC news quiz.

items.title: Penrith flyer breaks record in comeback Panthers win

children.content: Thomas Jenkins has claimed a slice of history in Penrith's unconvincing win over Parramatta in the western Sydney derby.

items.title: Queensland's 2011 floods inspire underground dance culture

children.content: Equal parts captivating and unsettling, young people from Ipswich to Japan are taking to the streets to dance "flood" style.

image.altText: A young man in a green bucket hat reaching towards the camera. He stands in a dimly lit hallway.

items.title: Probe launched into claims platypus burrow destroyed by council contractors

children.content: An investigation is launched after wildlife advocates say a platypus burrow was destroyed by an excavator being used for flood mitigation works in northern Tasmania.

image.altText: Wide shot of excavator and tree-felling works near river.

items.title: Big Build crime and corruption allegations warrant inquiry, says expert

children.content: One of Australia's leading public policy academics says a "Queensland-style" commission of inquiry is needed into Victoria's Big Build.

image.altText: the wind blows balck and yellow flags with yellow writing attached to fences of building sites with buildings behind

items.title: 'Prehistoric' hurdles keep this club's women's program from thriving

children.content: Macedon Football Netball Club is proud to do things its way. But it is in a pinch with its women and girls team.

gallery.altText: A woman with a scarf around her neck, on a dark sports field.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a scarf around her neck, on a dark sports field.

items.title: Fire brigade pushes for compensation after 22 members diagnosed with cancer

children.content: In Nar Nar Goon's fire brigade, 22 volunteers have been diagnosed with cancer. But as members work to obtain their work insurance cover, almost half of the members are not covered by the state's presumptive legislation.

image.altText: Two firefighters, and older man and a woman, standing side by side

items.title: Labor debates future, Liberals hold up pamphlet

children.content: Australia's two major parties are deliberating their futures this week with Labor's national conference and the Liberal Party's glossy new pamphlet.

image.altText: Albanese

items.title: The former cocaine-dealing Sydney socialite linked to a far-right news site

children.content: A "right of centre" website claiming to be news, with more than 42,000 Facebook followers, may not be as independent as it says. An ABC NEWS Verify investigation has found the site uses AI and a fake news editor.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair and a man with a black singlet and arm tattoos.

items.title: Bystanders praised for courage to stop attempted murder of woman

children.content: A man who "doggedly" tried to kill a woman in a prolonged attack in suburban Perth caught on CCTV has is been jailed for 16 years.

image.altText: Two men struggling on the ground.

items.title: Beefing up bobby calves for drive-through burgers

children.content: Dairy beef has caught the attention of fast food companies as farmers beef up bobby calves into viable assets.
